understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by positioning all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with '+' was occupied simultaneously; completing a level required all goals covered at once, and completing the game required winning the final level within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in that direction; walls or other blocking objects could prevent movement, while pushable blocks could be pushed if adjacent in the move direction and if space/interaction rules allowed. Multiple selectable pieces could exist; control switched by CLICKing the exact pixel-coordinate position of another selectable, but CLICK only worked when a selectable existed at that coordinate and selection changes were sometimes prevented by level rules/constraints. The movement_blocked flags were only hints (indicating adjacency) and did not strictly determine whether a move would succeed.

history.summary: Level 1 attempt 2/2 failed after 64 turns. No attempts remaining.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by positioning all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with '+' was occupied simultaneously; completing a level required all goals covered at once, and completing the game required winning the final level within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in that direction; walls or other blocking objects could prevent movement, while pushable blocks could be pushed if adjacent in the move direction and if space/interaction rules allowed. Multiple selectable pieces could exist; control switched by CLICKing the exact pixel-coordinate position of another selectable, but CLICK only worked when a selectable existed at that coordinate and selection changes were sometimes prevented by level rules/constraints. The movement_blocked flags were only hints (indicating adjacency) and did not strictly determine whether a move would succeed.
